parsed_claims.claim: Deprecated: import `upgradeWebSocket` and `websocket` directly from `hono/bun` instead.
parsed_claims.rationale: Marked with @deprecated tag indicating the current export should not be used; consumers must migrate to direct imports from hono/bun.

parsed_claims.claim: Defines the interface for execution context in a web worker or similar environment
parsed_claims.rationale: Describes the purpose of the interface as an abstraction over worker-like execution contexts
parsed_claims.excerpt: Interface for the execution context in a web worker or similar environment.

parsed_claims.claim: A sentinel USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT distinguishes 'unset' (use client default) from None (explicitly disable)
parsed_claims.rationale: None has semantic meaning (disable parameter), so a separate sentinel is needed to represent the unset state that defers to client defaults
parsed_claims.excerpt: we need to be able to indicate the default "unset" state, in a way that is distinctly different to using `None`

parsed_claims.claim: timeout=USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT defers to the client's configured timeout; timeout=None disables timeouts
parsed_claims.rationale: Illustrates the semantic difference between the sentinel and None in the request() signature
parsed_claims.excerpt: Omitting the `timeout` parameter will send a request using whatever default timeout has been configured on the client. Including `timeout=None` will ensure no timeout is used.

parsed_claims.claim: User code should not need to use the USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT constant; it is for internal use
parsed_claims.rationale: The sentinel is an internal implementation detail used when parameters are omitted
parsed_claims.excerpt: user code shouldn't need to use the `USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT` constant, but it is used internally when a parameter is not included

parsed_claims.claim: Serves as the default Authentication class used when no authentication is passed at the request level.
parsed_claims.rationale: Describes the role of this class as the fallback authentication mechanism.
parsed_claims.excerpt: Authentication class used when none is passed at the request-level.

parsed_claims.claim: See Authentication documentation for related details.

raw_response: {"claims":[{"symbol_candidates":["ExecuteContext","SetContext","Context"],"claim":"Context returns the underlying command context previously set via ExecuteContext or SetContext, or nil if none was set","severity":"context","rationale":"Documents the retrieval semantics for command context","excerpt":"Context returns underlying command context. If command was executed with ExecuteContext or the context was set with SetContext, the previously set context will be returned. Otherwise, nil is returned."},{"symbol_candidates":["Execute","ExecuteC","Context","context.Background"],"claim":"Execute and ExecuteC replace a nil command context with context.Background, so Context returns a background context after they run","severity":"soft","rationale":"Callers need to be aware that nil context is replaced after Execute/ExecuteC, affecting what Context returns","excerpt":"Notice that a call to Execute and ExecuteC will replace a nil context of a command with a context.Background, so a background context will be returned by Context after one of these functions has been called."}]}

parsed_claims.claim: Context returns the underlying command context previously set via ExecuteContext or SetContext, or nil if none was set
parsed_claims.rationale: Documents the retrieval semantics for command context
parsed_claims.excerpt: Context returns underlying command context. If command was executed with ExecuteContext or the context was set with SetContext, the previously set context will be returned. Otherwise, nil is returned.

parsed_claims.claim: Execute and ExecuteC replace a nil command context with context.Background, so Context returns a background context after they run
parsed_claims.rationale: Callers need to be aware that nil context is replaced after Execute/ExecuteC, affecting what Context returns
parsed_claims.excerpt: Notice that a call to Execute and ExecuteC will replace a nil context of a command with a context.Background, so a background context will be returned by Context after one of these functions has been called.

parsed_claims.claim: verify parameter accepts True (default CA bundle), False (disable verification), or an ssl.SSLContext instance for custom context
parsed_claims.rationale: The verify parameter encodes a security-relevant default for TLS verification
parsed_claims.excerpt: Either `True` to use an SSL context with the default CA bundle, `False` to disable verification, or an instance of `ssl.SSLContext` to use a custom context.

parsed_claims.claim: trust_env controls whether environment variables are used for configuration
